name: TikTok/YT Video + AI Story Audio Mashup
description: Generates a story from an input scenario using an LLM, downloads a TikTok video, converts the story to audio via TTS, waits for both to complete, then stitches the new audio with the downloaded video using FFmpeg.
tag: Content Creation, video get, tik tok

tool.name: Generate Story
tool.prompt:
Write a short, engaging story based on the following scenario:

{{Scenario}}

The story should be compelling, have a clear narrative arc, and be suitable for narration over a short video. Keep it concise — around 100-200 words.

Respond with only the story, not comments, title, or opinions.
tool.system_prompt: You are a creative storyteller. Write engaging, vivid short stories suitable for narration over video content. Keep the story concise enough to fit a short-form video (30-90 seconds when read aloud).

tool.name: Audio to SRT Transcription
tool.description: Converts an audio file into SRT (SubRip Subtitle) formatted text using OpenAI's Whisper speech recognition model. Returns only the SRT string.
tool.system_prompt: You are an audio transcription assistant. The user will provide an audio file. Use the transcribe_audio_to_srt function to convert it to SRT format. Return ONLY the raw SRT string output and nothing else — no explanation, no commentary, no markdown formatting.

agent_functions.name: transcribe_audio_to_srt
agent_functions.description: Transcribes an audio file to SRT subtitle format using OpenAI Whisper
agent_functions.function_string:
import os
import tempfile

def transcribe_audio_to_srt(file_path: str) -> str:
    """Transcribes an audio file and returns the result as an SRT formatted string.

    Args:
        file_path: Path to the audio file to transcribe.

    Returns:
        A string containing the transcription in SRT subtitle format.
    """
    try:
        import whisper

        if not os.path.exists(file_path):
            return f"Error: File not found at path: {file_path}"

        model = whisper.load_model("base")
        result = model.transcribe(file_path)

        segments = result.get("segments", [])
        if not segments:
            return "Error: No speech segments detected in the audio file."

        srt_lines = []
        for i, segment in enumerate(segments, start=1):
            start_time = segment["start"]
            end_time = segment["end"]
            text = segment["text"].strip()

            start_srt = _seconds_to_srt_time(start_time)
            end_srt = _seconds_to_srt_time(end_time)

            srt_lines.append(f"{i}")
            srt_lines.append(f"{start_srt} --> {end_srt}")
            srt_lines.append(text)
            srt_lines.append("")

        return "\n".join(srt_lines).strip()

    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error during transcription: {str(e)}"


def _seconds_to_srt_time(seconds: float) -> str:
    """Converts a float of seconds into SRT timestamp format HH:MM:SS,mmm.

    Args:
        seconds: Time value in seconds.

    Returns:
        A string in SRT time format (e.g. 00:01:23,456).
    """
    try:
        hours = int(seconds // 3600)
        minutes = int((seconds % 3600) // 60)
        secs = int(seconds % 60)
        millis = int(round((seconds - int(seconds)) * 1000))
        return f"{hours:02d}:{minutes:02d}:{secs:02d},{millis:03d}"
    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error converting time: {str(e)}"

request_inputs.name: Input
request_inputs.description: The audio file to transcribe into SRT format

tool.name: FFmpeg Tool
tool.description: Process audio and video with FFmpeg. Convert formats, extract audio, trim/cut/concatenate, resize, compress, add subtitles/watermarks, extract frames, apply filters, capture streams, and more. Includes a public command runner so arbitrary ffmpeg/ffprobe commands are executed (not just printed).
tool.prompt:
Using the file paths provided below, combine the downloaded TikTok video with the generated TTS audio. Replace the original audio in the video with the new TTS audio.

File information:
Video File: {{Download}}}}
TTS Audio: {{TTS.path}}
SRT: {{WorkingDirectory}}\sub.srt

Use FFmpeg to:
1. Take the video file from above
2. Replace its audio with the TTS audio file.  Loop or trim video to match audio length
3. Use the SRT data to add sub titles to the video in a stylistic way. Ensure subtitles fit in video (Font size:24)
4. Output a final combined video file {{WorkingDirectory}}\final_output.mp4

Complete requested work with no further   requests or questions.
tool.system_prompt:
You are an expert media processing assistant powered by FFmpeg.

Key requirement: You MUST actually execute ffmpeg/ffprobe commands via the provided public runner function(s). Do not merely print commands or ask the user to run them.

Workflow rules:
- Always verify input file paths exist before running ffmpeg.
- Prefer dedicated functions (convert/trim/etc.) when available.
- When using generic/escape-hatch commands, use run_ffmpeg/run_ffprobe which MUST execute the command.
- Always include -y for ffmpeg overwrites.
- Return clear results: what ran, stdout/stderr summary, and output paths.
- If a command fails, return the exit code and the last ~50 lines of stderr.
- Never expose internal/private functions (like _run). Use the public run_command/run_ffmpeg/run_ffprobe utilities.

agent_functions.name: FFmpegAgent
agent_functions.description: FFmpeg media processing utilities and robust public command execution helpers.

request_inputs.name: Input
request_inputs.description: User request describing the media task to perform, including any file paths and desired outputs.

tool.name: Save Text to File Path
tool.description: Receives a text string and a destination file path, then writes the text to that location (creating parent directories if needed).
tool.system_prompt:
You are a file-writing utility. Your job is to save the provided text content to the specified file path safely.

Requirements:
- Use the provided Content as the full file contents (no extra formatting).
- Write to FilePath exactly.
- Create parent directories if they do not exist.
- If FilePath points to an existing directory, return an error.
- Use UTF-8 encoding by default.
- Return a clear success message including the final absolute path and number of bytes written.
- If an error occurs (permissions, invalid path, etc.), return a clear error message.

Call the provided python function to perform the write operation.

agent_functions.name: file_writer
agent_functions.description: Utilities for safely writing text to disk.
agent_functions.function_string:
import os
from typing import Optional

def save_text_to_path(content: str, file_path: str, encoding: str = "utf-8") -> str:
    """Write the given text content to the specified file path.

    Creates parent directories if needed. Refuses to write if the path resolves to an existing directory.

    Args:
        content: The full text to write to the file.
        file_path: Destination path (absolute or relative).
        encoding: Text encoding to use (default utf-8).

    Returns:
        A success or error message.
    """
    try:
        if file_path is None or str(file_path).strip() == "":
            return "ERROR: FilePath is required and cannot be empty."

        # Normalize path
        expanded = os.path.expanduser(str(file_path))
        abs_path = os.path.abspath(expanded)

        # If path exists and is a directory, refuse
        if os.path.exists(abs_path) and os.path.isdir(abs_path):
            return f"ERROR: The destination path points to an existing directory: {abs_path}"

        parent_dir = os.path.dirname(abs_path) or "."
        if parent_dir and not os.path.exists(parent_dir):
            os.makedirs(parent_dir, exist_ok=True)

        # Write file
        data = "" if content is None else str(content)
        with open(abs_path, "w", encoding=encoding, newline="") as f:
            f.write(data)

        bytes_written = len(data.encode(encoding, errors="replace"))
        return f"OK: Wrote {bytes_written} bytes to {abs_path}"
    except PermissionError as e:
        return f"ERROR: Permission denied writing to '{file_path}': {e}"
    except OSError as e:
        return f"ERROR: OS error writing to '{file_path}': {e}"
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERROR: Unexpected error: {e}"

request_inputs.name: Content
request_inputs.description: Text content to save into the file.

request_inputs.name: FilePath
request_inputs.description: Destination file path where the content should be saved (absolute or relative).

tool.name: TikTok/YT Video Downloader
tool.description: Downloads TikTok videos from a URL using yt-dlp. Validates the URL, creates the output directory if needed, downloads the video file, and returns the file path with metadata (title, uploader, duration, file size). Requires a TikTok video URL and output directory path. Optionally accepts a custom filename to save the video as.
tool.system_prompt:
You are a TikTok video downloader assistant. When the user provides a TikTok URL, use the download_tiktok_video function to download the video. 

Instructions:
- Extract the TikTok URL from the user's message
- Call the download function with the URL and the output directory: {{Output Directory}}
- If the user provided a custom filename, pass it as the `name` parameter. If not, pass an empty string or omit it to use the default video title.
- Report the result back to the user, including the file path if successful
- If the user doesn't provide a valid TikTok URL, ask them to provide one

Only download videos from TikTok URLs (e.g., URLs containing tiktok.com).

agent_functions.name: tiktok_downloader
agent_functions.description: Downloads TikTok videos given a URL and saves them to a specified output directory, with an optional custom filename.
agent_functions.function_string:
import os
import json

def download_tiktok_video(url: str, output_dir: str, name: str = "") -> str:
    """Download a TikTok video from the given URL and save it to the specified directory.
    
    Args:
        url: The TikTok video URL to download.
        output_dir: The directory path where the downloaded video will be saved.
        name: Optional custom filename (without extension). If empty, the video title is used.
    
    Returns:
        A string with the result including file path and metadata, or an error message.
    """
    try:
        import yt_dlp
    except ImportError:
        return "Error: yt-dlp is not installed. Please install it with 'pip install yt-dlp'."

    # Validate URL
    if not url or not isinstance(url, str):
        return "Error: Please provide a valid TikTok video URL."
    
    url = url.strip()
    if 'tiktok.com' not in url.lower():
        return f"Error: The URL '{url}' does not appear to be a TikTok URL. Please provide a URL containing 'tiktok.com'."

    # Create output directory if it doesn't exist
    try:
        os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error: Could not create output directory '{output_dir}': {str(e)}"

    # Determine the output template
    if name and name.strip():
        # Use the custom name provided by the user (sanitize basic path separators)
        safe_name = name.strip().replace('/', '_').replace('\\', '_')
        # Remove .mp4 extension if user included it, we'll add it via format
        if safe_name.lower().endswith('.mp4'):
            safe_name = safe_name[:-4]
        outtmpl = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.%(ext)s")
    else:
        # Default: use the video title
        outtmpl = os.path.join(output_dir, '%(title)s.%(ext)s')

    ydl_opts = {
        'outtmpl': outtmpl,
        'format': 'best[ext=mp4]/best',
        'noplaylist': True,
        'quiet': True,
        'no_warnings': True,
        'socket_timeout': 30,
        'retries': 3,
        'merge_output_format': 'mp4',
    }

    try:
        with yt_dlp.YoutubeDL(ydl_opts) as ydl:
            # Extract info first to get metadata
            info = ydl.extract_info(url, download=True)
            
            if info is None:
                return "Error: Could not extract video information. The URL may be invalid or the video may be unavailable."

            # Determine the actual filename that was saved
            if name and name.strip():
                safe_name = name.strip().replace('/', '_').replace('\\', '_')
                if safe_name.lower().endswith('.mp4'):
                    safe_name = safe_name[:-4]
                filename = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.mp4")
            else:
                filename = ydl.prepare_filename(info)
                # Ensure .mp4 extension
                base, ext = os.path.splitext(filename)
                if ext != '.mp4':
                    mp4_path = base + '.mp4'
                    if os.path.exists(mp4_path):
                        filename = mp4_path

            # Gather metadata
            title = info.get('title', 'Unknown')
            uploader = info.get('uploader', 'Unknown')
            duration = info.get('duration', 0)
            
            # Get file size
            file_size = 0
            if os.path.exists(filename):
                file_size = os.path.getsize(filename)
                file_size_mb = round(file_size / (1024 * 1024), 2)
            else:
                # Try to find the file with a glob pattern
                import glob
                pattern = os.path.join(output_dir, '*')
                files = sorted(glob.glob(pattern), key=os.path.getmtime, reverse=True)
                if files:
                    filename = files[0]
                    file_size = os.path.getsize(filename)
                    file_size_mb = round(file_size / (1024 * 1024), 2)
                else:
                    return "Error: Download seemed to succeed but the file could not be found."

            # Format duration
            if duration:
                minutes = int(duration) // 60
                seconds = int(duration) % 60
                duration_str = f"{minutes}m {seconds}s"
            else:
                duration_str = "Unknown"

            result = (
                f"✅ Video downloaded successfully!\n\n"
                f"📁 File Path: {filename}\n"
                f"📝 Title: {title}\n"
                f"👤 Uploader: {uploader}\n"
                f"⏱️ Duration: {duration_str}\n"
                f"💾 File Size: {file_size_mb} MB"
            )
            return result

    except Exception as e:
        error_msg = str(e)
        if 'HTTP Error 404' in error_msg:
            return "Error: Video not found (404). The video may have been deleted or the URL is incorrect."
        elif 'Private video' in error_msg or 'private' in error_msg.lower():
            return "Error: This video is private and cannot be downloaded."
        elif 'HTTP Error 429' in error_msg:
            return "Error: Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again."
        else:
            return f"Error downloading video: {error_msg}"

request_inputs.name: Input
request_inputs.description: The TikTok video URL to download

request_inputs.name: Output Directory
request_inputs.description: The directory path where the downloaded video will be saved

request_inputs.name: Filename
request_inputs.description: Optional custom filename for the saved video (without extension). If not provided, the video title will be used.

tool.name: ElevenLabs TTS
tool.description: Generate speech audio from text using the ElevenLabs API. Supports voice selection, model choice, and returns the generated audio as a playable file.
tool.prompt:
generate this text "{{Input}}".
using this voice id "{{Voice}}".
Do not add pre or post dialog, only return the path to the generated audio file
tool.system_prompt:
You are an assistant that helps users generate speech audio using the ElevenLabs text-to-speech API. You have access to tools that can:

1. List available voices so the user can pick one
2. Generate speech from text with a chosen voice
3. List available models

When the user provides text and a voice preference, use the tools to generate audio. If they don't specify a voice, list available voices first and suggest a good default (e.g. "Rachel" or the first available voice). If they provide a voice name, search for it in the available voices.

Always confirm what was generated and provide the output file path. If the user just wants to list voices or models, do that directly.

agent_functions.name: elevenlabs_tools
agent_functions.description: Functions for interacting with the ElevenLabs text-to-speech API: list voices, list models, and generate speech audio.

request_inputs.name: Input
request_inputs.description: The text you want to convert to speech, or a command like 'list voices'

request_inputs.name: Voice
request_inputs.description: Voice name to use (e.g. Rachel, Adam, Bella). Leave empty to use the default or list available voices.

env_variables.name: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY
env_variables.description: Your ElevenLabs API key. Get it from https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys

import os
import tempfile

def transcribe_audio_to_srt(file_path: str) -> str:
    """Transcribes an audio file and returns the result as an SRT formatted string.

    Args:
        file_path: Path to the audio file to transcribe.

    Returns:
        A string containing the transcription in SRT subtitle format.
    """
    try:
        import whisper

        if not os.path.exists(file_path):
            return f"Error: File not found at path: {file_path}"

        model = whisper.load_model("base")
        result = model.transcribe(file_path)

        segments = result.get("segments", [])
        if not segments:
            return "Error: No speech segments detected in the audio file."

        srt_lines = []
        for i, segment in enumerate(segments, start=1):
            start_time = segment["start"]
            end_time = segment["end"]
            text = segment["text"].strip()

            start_srt = _seconds_to_srt_time(start_time)
            end_srt = _seconds_to_srt_time(end_time)

            srt_lines.append(f"{i}")
            srt_lines.append(f"{start_srt} --> {end_srt}")
            srt_lines.append(text)
            srt_lines.append("")

        return "\n".join(srt_lines).strip()

    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error during transcription: {str(e)}"


def _seconds_to_srt_time(seconds: float) -> str:
    """Converts a float of seconds into SRT timestamp format HH:MM:SS,mmm.

    Args:
        seconds: Time value in seconds.

    Returns:
        A string in SRT time format (e.g. 00:01:23,456).
    """
    try:
        hours = int(seconds // 3600)
        minutes = int((seconds % 3600) // 60)
        secs = int(seconds % 60)
        millis = int(round((seconds - int(seconds)) * 1000))
        return f"{hours:02d}:{minutes:02d}:{secs:02d},{millis:03d}"
    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error converting time: {str(e)}"

import os
from typing import Optional

def save_text_to_path(content: str, file_path: str, encoding: str = "utf-8") -> str:
    """Write the given text content to the specified file path.

    Creates parent directories if needed. Refuses to write if the path resolves to an existing directory.

    Args:
        content: The full text to write to the file.
        file_path: Destination path (absolute or relative).
        encoding: Text encoding to use (default utf-8).

    Returns:
        A success or error message.
    """
    try:
        if file_path is None or str(file_path).strip() == "":
            return "ERROR: FilePath is required and cannot be empty."

        # Normalize path
        expanded = os.path.expanduser(str(file_path))
        abs_path = os.path.abspath(expanded)

        # If path exists and is a directory, refuse
        if os.path.exists(abs_path) and os.path.isdir(abs_path):
            return f"ERROR: The destination path points to an existing directory: {abs_path}"

        parent_dir = os.path.dirname(abs_path) or "."
        if parent_dir and not os.path.exists(parent_dir):
            os.makedirs(parent_dir, exist_ok=True)

        # Write file
        data = "" if content is None else str(content)
        with open(abs_path, "w", encoding=encoding, newline="") as f:
            f.write(data)

        bytes_written = len(data.encode(encoding, errors="replace"))
        return f"OK: Wrote {bytes_written} bytes to {abs_path}"
    except PermissionError as e:
        return f"ERROR: Permission denied writing to '{file_path}': {e}"
    except OSError as e:
        return f"ERROR: OS error writing to '{file_path}': {e}"
    except Exception as e:
        return f"ERROR: Unexpected error: {e}"

agent_functions.function_string:
import os
import json

def download_tiktok_video(url: str, output_dir: str, name: str = "") -> str:
    """Download a TikTok video from the given URL and save it to the specified directory.
    
    Args:
        url: The TikTok video URL to download.
        output_dir: The directory path where the downloaded video will be saved.
        name: Optional custom filename (without extension). If empty, the video title is used.
    
    Returns:
        A string with the result including file path and metadata, or an error message.
    """
    try:
        import yt_dlp
    except ImportError:
        return "Error: yt-dlp is not installed. Please install it with 'pip install yt-dlp'."

    # Validate URL
    if not url or not isinstance(url, str):
        return "Error: Please provide a valid TikTok video URL."
    
    url = url.strip()
    if 'tiktok.com' not in url.lower():
        return f"Error: The URL '{url}' does not appear to be a TikTok URL. Please provide a URL containing 'tiktok.com'."

    # Create output directory if it doesn't exist
    try:
        os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error: Could not create output directory '{output_dir}': {str(e)}"

    # Determine the output template
    if name and name.strip():
        # Use the custom name provided by the user (sanitize basic path separators)
        safe_name = name.strip().replace('/', '_').replace('\\', '_')
        # Remove .mp4 extension if user included it, we'll add it via format
        if safe_name.lower().endswith('.mp4'):
            safe_name = safe_name[:-4]
        outtmpl = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.%(ext)s")
    else:
        # Default: use the video title
        outtmpl = os.path.join(output_dir, '%(title)s.%(ext)s')

    ydl_opts = {
        'outtmpl': outtmpl,
        'format': 'best[ext=mp4]/best',
        'noplaylist': True,
        'quiet': True,
        'no_warnings': True,
        'socket_timeout': 30,
        'retries': 3,
        'merge_output_format': 'mp4',
    }

    try:
        with yt_dlp.YoutubeDL(ydl_opts) as ydl:
            # Extract info first to get metadata
            info = ydl.extract_info(url, download=True)
            
            if info is None:
                return "Error: Could not extract video information. The URL may be invalid or the video may be unavailable."

            # Determine the actual filename that was saved
            if name and name.strip():
                safe_name = name.strip().replace('/', '_').replace('\\', '_')
                if safe_name.lower().endswith('.mp4'):
                    safe_name = safe_name[:-4]
                filename = os.path.join(output_dir, f"{safe_name}.mp4")
            else:
                filename = ydl.prepare_filename(info)
                # Ensure .mp4 extension
                base, ext = os.path.splitext(filename)
                if ext != '.mp4':
                    mp4_path = base + '.mp4'
                    if os.path.exists(mp4_path):
                        filename = mp4_path

            # Gather metadata
            title = info.get('title', 'Unknown')
            uploader = info.get('uploader', 'Unknown')
            duration = info.get('duration', 0)
            
            # Get file size
            file_size = 0
            if os.path.exists(filename):
                file_size = os.path.getsize(filename)
                file_size_mb = round(file_size / (1024 * 1024), 2)
            else:
                # Try to find the file with a glob pattern
                import glob
                pattern = os.path.join(output_dir, '*')
                files = sorted(glob.glob(pattern), key=os.path.getmtime, reverse=True)
                if files:
                    filename = files[0]
                    file_size = os.path.getsize(filename)
                    file_size_mb = round(file_size / (1024 * 1024), 2)
                else:
                    return "Error: Download seemed to succeed but the file could not be found."

            # Format duration
            if duration:
                minutes = int(duration) // 60
                seconds = int(duration) % 60
                duration_str = f"{minutes}m {seconds}s"
            else:
                duration_str = "Unknown"

            result = (
                f"✅ Video downloaded successfully!\n\n"
                f"📁 File Path: {filename}\n"
                f"📝 Title: {title}\n"
                f"👤 Uploader: {uploader}\n"
                f"⏱️ Duration: {duration_str}\n"
                f"💾 File Size: {file_size_mb} MB"
            )
            return result

    except Exception as e:
        error_msg = str(e)
        if 'HTTP Error 404' in error_msg:
            return "Error: Video not found (404). The video may have been deleted or the URL is incorrect."
        elif 'Private video' in error_msg or 'private' in error_msg.lower():
            return "Error: This video is private and cannot be downloaded."
        elif 'HTTP Error 429' in error_msg:
            return "Error: Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again."
        else:
            return f"Error downloading video: {error_msg}"

request_inputs.name: Input
request_inputs.description: The TikTok video URL to download

request_inputs.name: Output Directory
request_inputs.description: The directory path where the downloaded video will be saved

request_inputs.name: Filename
request_inputs.description: Optional custom filename for the saved video (without extension). If not provided, the video title will be used.

_tools.name: ElevenLabs TTS
_tools.description: Generate speech audio from text using the ElevenLabs API. Supports voice selection, model choice, and returns the generated audio as a playable file.
_tools.prompt:
generate this text "{{Input}}".
using this voice id "{{Voice}}".
Do not add pre or post dialog, only return the path to the generated audio file
_tools.system_prompt:
You are an assistant that helps users generate speech audio using the ElevenLabs text-to-speech API. You have access to tools that can:

1. List available voices so the user can pick one
2. Generate speech from text with a chosen voice
3. List available models

When the user provides text and a voice preference, use the tools to generate audio. If they don't specify a voice, list available voices first and suggest a good default (e.g. "Rachel" or the first available voice). If they provide a voice name, search for it in the available voices.

Always confirm what was generated and provide the output file path. If the user just wants to list voices or models, do that directly.

agent_functions.name: elevenlabs_tools
agent_functions.description: Functions for interacting with the ElevenLabs text-to-speech API: list voices, list models, and generate speech audio.

request_inputs.name: Input
request_inputs.description: The text you want to convert to speech, or a command like 'list voices'

request_inputs.name: Voice
request_inputs.description: Voice name to use (e.g. Rachel, Adam, Bella). Leave empty to use the default or list available voices.

env_variables.name: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY
env_variables.description: Your ElevenLabs API key. Get it from https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys
